svg.font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
svg.title: Scaling the audit chain: sharded per-entity chains, Merkle batching, and ledger anchoring
svg.desc: Usage and audit events arrive at high volume. Instead of one global hash chain, the system writes sharded per-entity chains so writes proceed in parallel with no global bottleneck. Event hashes are then batched into Merkle trees, producing one root per batch. Only the roots are anchored into the immutable ledger owned by work package S14, which lets an auditor verify a single segment rather than replaying the entire history.
svg.text: Every access logged, append only, tokens not PII
Parallel writes, no global serialization point
One root per batch, not one anchor per event
Verify a segment via Merkle proof, not all history
Signed checkpoints bound verification cost independently of chain length
